zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Clifford於1307年出生在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德阿波萊比時，她的父親Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford33歲，母親Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles31歲。她和Piers de Mauley至少有1個兒子。她去世於1382年8月8日在英國英格蘭盧特藍德哈布萊頓享年75歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Clifford was born in 1307, in Appleby, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, was 33 and her mother, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, was 31. She had at least 1 son with Piers de Mauley. She died on 8 August 1382, in Hambleton, Rutland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Clifford est née en 1307, à Appleby, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, avait 31 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Piers de Mauley. Elle est décédée le 8 août 1382, à Hambleton, Rutland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Clifford 1307 in Appleby, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford 33 und ihre Mutter Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles 31. Sie hatte mit Piers de Mauley mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 8. August 1382 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Hambleton, Rutland, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Clifford è nata il nel 1307 nel Appleby, Westmorland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, aveva 31 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Piers de Mauley. Morì il 8 agosto 1382, nel Hambleton, Rutland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 75 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Cliffordが1307年にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドアップレビーで生まれた時，父Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Cliffordは33歳，母Maud de Clare Baroness de Wellesは31歳であった。彼女はPiers de Mauleyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1382年8月8日にイギリスイギリスルトランドハンブレトンで75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clifford(이)가 1307년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 애플레비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 Piers de Mauley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1382년 8월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 루틀랜드 햄블레턴에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Clifford nasceu em 1307, em Appleby, Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, tinha 31 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Piers de Mauley. Ela faleceu em 8 de agosto de 1382, em Hambleton, Rutland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 75 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Clifford родилась в 1307 г. в местности эпплеби, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, 31. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Piers de Mauley. Она умерла 8 августа 1382 г. в местности хамблетон, рутланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Clifford nació en 1307, en Appleby, Westmorland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Piers de Mauley. Murió el 8 de agosto de 1382, en Hambleton, Rutland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 75 años.
